payload.summary: Planned a kill-switchable cross-vendor cost-arbitrage learning instrument. Instrument-first: durable five-family signal substrate + metered-counterfactual ledger + two active probes, with a deliberately simple static-priority router behind a hard/soft constraint split. Coordinated tier, 5-package DAG.

decisions.title: Bounded instrument capability (Approach A)
decisions.rationale: Only option honouring the kill-switch principle: one capability, one feature flag, clean removal seam, slot-in point for the deferred smart router.

decisions.title: Signals ride audit_log + OTel; interpretive records extend roadmap-runtime
decisions.rationale: Reuse append-only immutability + graceful-degrade (audit/telemetry) and existing cost/decision schema fields (LearningEntry.vendor_notes, checkpoint.vendor_state). No new tables.

decisions.title: Hard constraints as mutable Cedar forbid() policies
decisions.rationale: EULA/eligibility is a moving input; a config edit must reorganise routing without redeploy (NOTIFY-invalidated).

decisions.title: Cost model = static price table + adapter tokens with labelled estimates
decisions.rationale: No token tracking exists; degrade to labelled estimates rather than block the ledger. Headline metric reportable with/without estimates.

next_steps: Implement wp-contracts first (schemas + fixtures), then wp-coordinator-signals.
Run wp-probes and wp-skill-router in parallel after the signal substrate lands.
